step_by_step_thinking:
Given the information that the patient has amenorrhea lasting 3 months and both LH and FSH levels are elevated threefold above normal values, we need to evaluate potential causes related to hypo-or hyper-gonadotropic states. Elevated FSH and LH suggest either a failure in the feedback mechanism leading to primary ovarian insufficiency (POI) or some form of premature ovarian aging/dysfunction. Checking serum estradiol could clarify whether this is associated with reduced estrogen production typical of POI, thus guiding management towards supportive care versus exploring alternative treatments such as hormone replacement therapy.
\boxed{\text{B}}
```markdown
Given the evidence showing high LH and FSH levels, checking serum estradiol would help determine if there is concurrent reduction in estrogens suggestive of primary ovarian insufficiency.
\boxed{\text{B}}
```

